sessions.intent: Design causal callstack: App.Debug-owned, off by default, auto-materialized on error, render-agnostic data for trace + flamegraph projections.

before.assumptions: Callstack code in App/CallStack/ is partly wired: App.Run pushes/pops frames per action, but frame.Error is never set on failure, CallStack.Errors is never populated, !error reads from a parallel Context.Error property, and IsEnabled=false has incoherent push/pop. ErrorChain already exists on Error. Callback design is settled-enough to know it imposes only OBP discipline (frames stay by-ref) on this branch.
before.risk: Recovery scope semantics, %!error% walk-back rule, and the Context.Error -> walk-the-stack transition could regress LlmFixer/error.handle behavior if not migrated carefully. Variable diff capture must not reintroduce the OOM that 10000x10MB foreach previously triggered (mitigated by callstack-off-by-default plus scalar-by-default diff capture).

actions.context: Appended Callback context entry per user request. Captures concept, settled-with-user design points (developer-declared vars, encrypted on wire, sign goal_hash), and three constraints Callback imposes on the callstack branch: (1) Frame stays OBP - no denormalized IDs, (2) Variable snapshot is Callback's concern not callstack's, (3) No Data.Pause lane needed in callstack work. Out-of-scope flag preserved for Callback's own future branch.

Full plan after multi-turn design discussion with user. Covers ownership move (Context -> Debug), rename (CallFrame -> Call.@this), causal Cause link, Option B recovery (synthetic frame), Option X sibling-split (temporal compression), %!error% via stack-walk, Variables.Events for diffs, Tags from both C# and PLang sides, auto-materialization from Action.Step.Goal.Parent walk on error, scalar-by-default diff capture to avoid prior OOM scenario, render-agnostic data so flamegraph projection falls out for free. 10 phases, file map, risk register, 10 PLang tests + 4 C# test suites listed. Callback explicitly out of scope, recorded in todos for future branch.

Architect session and bot-root summaries. v1/summary.md describes settled decisions, the new App.Run shape as code example, and the next-bot recommendation (test-designer). Bot-root summary.md is the cross-session light index.
Initial session report for the runtime2-callstack branch.

after.notes: One implementation-time decision deferred to coder: synthetic recovery frame's Action reference (reuse recovery goal first action vs sentinel RecoveryAction type) - decide when OBP shape becomes concrete. All architectural decisions settled with user across the multi-turn discussion: causal model, recovery Option B, sibling-split Option X, Frame->Call rename, App.Debug ownership, --debug JSON config shape, scalar-by-default diffs, ErrorChain integration. Callback context captured in todos.md so its future design knows the callstack contract.

sessions.intent: Spec test contract for the causal callstack: Call.@this shape, AsyncLocal forking, Cause linkage, recovery via Cause, Variables collection events / diff capture, app.Errors.Push scope, tag action, --debug={callstack:{...}} parse, cycle detection.

before.assumptions: Architect's plan in v1/plan.md is settled. Existing PLang.Tests/App/Core/CallStackTests.cs targets the OLD shape (IsEnabled, Frame.Parent, EventId, Phase) and will be rewritten/deleted by coder. New tests live under PLang.Tests/App/CallStack/, PLang.Tests/App/Errors/, PLang.Tests/App/Variables/, PLang.Tests/App/Debug/, PLang.Tests/App/Modules/debug/. Existing Tests/App/CallStack/ has thin %!callStack.Depth% goals that will also be rewritten under the new tree-walk semantics.
before.risk: Two PLang tests (P8 OOM safety, P9 cancellation) may not be expressible via plang --test alone. P8 likely belongs in C# (RSS measurement); P9 may need a runtime cancel hook or move to C# side. Will flag in the relevant batch and ask user to decide before writing files.

actions.context: C# TUnit stubs (~70 tests) covering the new Call.@this shape, tree mechanics, AsyncLocal fork isolation, Cause distinct from Caller, MaxDepth/ContainsGoal cycle detection, CallStackFlags record struct, Items<T> typed bag, Variables.OnSet-driven diff capture incl. memory-bound test (absorbed P8 from PLang side), run-wide Audit accumulator, and SnapshotChain leaf-to-root ordering with chain[0]==self. All bodies are Assert.Fail("Not implemented").

C# TUnit stubs (~28 tests) for the supporting surfaces: app.Errors.Push LIFO + AsyncLocal flow, ServiceError.CallFrames typed-shape + chain[0]==failing-call invariant, Variables collection-level OnSet/OnCreate/OnRemove with back-compat for per-variable events, the new `tag` action handler (Pairs and Label forms, no-op when Current null, not cacheable), and --debug={callstack:{...}} JSON parse (shorthand, full object, partial, defaults, bad-JSON fallback).

16 PLang test goals — one goal per file per project rule. Cover depth tracking, cross-file chain, Audit accumulator, %!error% nesting incl. null-outside-scope, Cause linkage in recovery body, diff capture in --debug={callstack:{diff:true}}, timeout-modifier-as-cancel-channel (replaces P9 cancel primitive), direct/indirect cycle detection, tag action both Pairs and Label forms, Handled flag both success and failure cases. All bodies are `- throw "not implemented"`.

Removed obsolete CallStack tests that targeted the deprecated `%!callStack.Depth%` property. The new shape exposes Caller/Children walks instead. Old .build/ cache also removed; regenerates on next build.
Standard test-designer artefacts: plan.md, finalized test-plan.md (file-by-file count + decisions made), v1 summary, verdict.json {pass:true}, bot-root summary index entry.

after.notes: Existing PLang.Tests/App/Core/CallStackTests.cs (against old IsEnabled/Frame.Parent/EventId/Phase shape) intentionally NOT touched — coder will delete/replace it during migration. Two design choices recorded with user approval: P8 OOM safety moved to C# (DiffCaptureTests memory-bound test), P9 cancellation expressed via existing `timeout after` modifier rather than introducing a new cancel primitive. Per-batch interactive flow short-circuited after batch 4 at user request.

sessions.intent: Five-pass simplicity/OBP/behavioral review of coder/v1 callstack refactor.

before.assumptions: coder/v1 lands AsyncLocal Call tree, Errors namespace, Cause linkage, Variables collection events, tag action, --debug={callstack:...}; C# tests pass (2580/2580); PLang tests blocked on pre-existing builder issue.
before.risk: Concurrent paths (Task.WhenAll branches sharing a Variables) and the new exception flow (CallStackOverflowException vs App.Run try/catch) most likely to harbor surprise behavior.

actions.context: Codeanalyzer artefacts: plan, full result.md (19 findings, 0 MAJOR, 5 MINOR-with-fix-recommendation, ~14 NITs), verdict.json {status:fail}, v1 summary, bot-root summary index.

Five-pass analysis: OBP, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning, deletion test. Key findings: (1) CallStackFlags.Tags documented but never checked - tag writes unconditional. (2) Errors._current is static AsyncLocal on instance class - inconsistent with CallStack which deliberately moved to instance for test isolation. (3) stack.Push runs OUTSIDE App.Run try/catch - CallStackOverflowException escapes the exception->Data contract. (4) Call.Diffs.Add not thread-safe; OnSet fired without lock; parallel branches sharing Variables race on List<T>. (5) context.Step.Context = context mutates shared Step, not restored in finally. (6) SerializableCallStack.cs is dead code - zero refs in PLang/, only test GlobalUsings aliases. NITs: depth recomputed three times per Push (cycle-check, ContainsGoal, Call.Depth), CallStackOverflowException reuses depth-message for goal-cycle, GoalFirst/RetryFirst branches duplicate structure.

after.health: no MAJOR; 5 MINOR fixes recommended before tester
after.notes: Send back to coder for #1 (Diffs race), #10 (Tags flag), #11 (Errors static AsyncLocal), #13 (Push exception escape), #14 (Step.Context mutation). NITs optional. After fixes: tester.
